title: primula elatior subsp. elatior
intro: Primula elatior subsp. elatior, also known as the greater primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant has been found to possess medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Primula elatior subsp. elatior include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Primula elatior subsp. elatior is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be applied topically to the skin as a paste or salve.
